Title: MUR1-mediated cell-wall fucosylation is required for freezing tolerance in Arabidopsis thaliana

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BOR1, a boron transporter, is required under boron-limiting conditions for normal RGII borate-mediated dimerization in leaf cell walls. 2. bor1-3 mutants showed reduced RGII dimerization and increased freeze-induced electrolyte leakage, linking boron transport to basal freezing tolerance. 3. The data support that impaired freezing tolerance can arise from defective RGII crosslinking even without defects in fucose biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in root cells; contribution to RGII dimerization appears stronger in aerial/rosette tissues under low boron based on mutant phenotype discussed in this study
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in root cells under boron-related function
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve stress resilience on boron-limited soils by maintaining cell-wall integrity and RGII crosslinking; should be combined with boron nutrition management; caution that benefit is context dependent on boron availability
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Rhamnogalacturonan II borate-crosslinked dimer (RGII dimerization status)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall of leaf/rosette tissues
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BOR2; RGII (functional connection via boron supply)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because direct mutant analysis in the Results linked BOR1-dependent boron transport to RGII dimerization and freezing tolerance. It is not a pathway enzyme or transcriptional regulator.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (bor1-3 and bor1-3 bor2-1); electrolyte leakage assay after freezing; RGII monomer/dimer analysis by polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis under boron-limiting conditions
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cell-wall structural integrity and contributes to basal freezing tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of BOR1 reduces RGII dimerization and increases freezing sensitivity under boron limitation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants require supplementary boric acid to maintain normal growth and cell-wall structure; reduced growth/cell-wall integrity under low boron
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Boron-limiting growth conditions; freezing treatment without prior cold acclimation

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BOR2, a boron transporter, contributes to boron-dependent RGII crosslinking and freezing tolerance. 2. The bor1-3bor2-1 double mutant displayed reduced RGII dimerization and elevated freeze-induced electrolyte leakage. 3. These results support a causal connection between boron transport, cell-wall RGII crosslinking, and basal freezing tolerance.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in root cells in prior characterization referenced by the paper
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in root cells under boron-related function
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering/breeding target for maintaining cell-wall boron homeostasis and stress tolerance on boron-deficient soils; likely best deployed with BOR1 or boron management strategies
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Rhamnogalacturonan II borate-crosslinked dimer (RGII dimerization status)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall of leaf/rosette tissues
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BOR1; RGII (functional connection via boron supply)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The single bor2-1 effect on leaf RGII dimerization was weaker than bor1-3 in this study, but BOR2 remained part of the experimentally tested boron transport module affecting freezing tolerance, especially in the double mutant.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (bor2-1 and bor1-3 bor2-1); electrolyte leakage assay after freezing; RGII monomer/dimer analysis by polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cell-wall structure and contributes to freezing tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defects in BOR2, especially combined with bor1-3, impair RGII dimerization and increase freezing sensitivity under boron limitation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced growth/cell-wall integrity under low boron reported for boron transporter mutants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Boron-limiting growth conditions; freezing treatment without prior cold acclimation

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; sfr8/mur1 mutant lines; complemented sfr8-C line
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: GDP-L-fucose biosynthesis; cell-wall polysaccharide fucosylation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: De novo activated fucose biosynthesis for cell-wall fucosylation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream; first step in de novo GDP-L-fucose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step/not formally established as rate-limiting; first committed enzymatic step toward GDP-L-fucose production in the study context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GDP-D-mannose into a 4-keto-6-deoxy GDP-sugar intermediate in the first step of de novo GDP-L-fucose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool required for fucosylation of RGII pectin and consequent borate-mediated RGII dimerization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cell-wall fucose / fucosylated RGII and RGII borate-crosslinked dimerization status
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall, especially leaf/rosette tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains cell-wall structure and confers basal freezing tolerance through efficient RGII dimerization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not induced by low temperature or cold acclimation; not upregulated by CBF2/CBF3 overexpression; functions in basal state
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not induced by exposure to 5°C for 1 h to 7 d according to qPCR and cited transcriptomic datasets
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S CaMV promoter (constitutive) used for complementation in transgenic sfr8-C plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sfr8 was mapped to MUR1/At3g51160, and mur1 alleles phenocopied sfr8 for freezing sensitivity. 2. MUR1 encodes GDP-D-mannose 4,6-dehydratase required for GDP-L-fucose biosynthesis; sfr8 extracts failed to convert GDP-D-mannose to GDP-L-fucose. 3. MUR1 loss reduced cell-wall fucose incorporation and RGII dimerization, causing increased freezing sensitivity. 4. Constitutive complementation with MUR1 restored cell-wall fucose, RGII dimerization, and freezing tolerance. 5. Boric acid supplementation rescued freezing sensitivity of mur1/sfr8, supporting that the key metabolic consequence is defective RGII borate crosslinking.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cell-wall fucosylation and RGII dimerization, thereby increasing freezing tolerance; loss of function causes low wall fucose and freezing sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fine mapping; whole-genome sequencing; allelism with mur1 EMS alleles; biochemical conversion assay from GDP-D-mannose to GDP-L-fucose; cell-wall sugar GC-MS; transgenic complementation under 35S promoter; RGII monomer/dimer electrophoresis; electrolyte leakage freezing assays; boric acid rescue
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss-of-function mutants show rounded/cup-shaped leaves, reduced petiole length, altered growth and reduced mechanical strength; phenotype depends partly on boron availability
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering cell-wall integrity and freezing tolerance via maintaining GDP-L-fucose supply and RGII crosslinking; deployment may require adequate boron nutrition; caution that broad cell-wall pleiotropy can affect morphology and growth
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Illumina whole-genome sequencing of sfr8 genomic DNA mapped to TAIR10 using Bowtie/Galaxy; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: sfr8 is identical to previously described mur1-4. The paper argues MUR1 contributes mainly to basal rather than cold-induced freezing tolerance. MUR1 is a pathway gene although the terminal trait is stress tolerance rather than nutritional enhancement.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Short-chain dehydratase/reductase family; GDP-D-mannose-4,6-dehydratase isoform GMD2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold acclimation; freezing stress; boric acid supplementation; exogenous fucose supplementation; 2-fluoro-fucose inhibition context
